name: Luarca to Tineo Cycling Route
description: This difficult 55.5-mile (89.3 km) road cycling route from Navelgas to Luarca offers challenging climbs and diverse Asturian scenery.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Tineo, nestled in Asturias, Spain, traverse a diverse and challenging terrain characterized by hilly and mountainous topography. The region features continuous ascents and descents, offering panoramic views across lush greenery and river valleys. Cyclists can expect well-paved, low-traffic roads that wind through both natural landscapes and historic towns, making it a compelling destination for active exploration. Best road cycling routes around Tineo The most popular road cycling route is Alto de la Marta and Alto de Porciles Loop from Navelgas, a 60.3 miles (97.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route features challenging climbs alongside diverse Asturian scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tineo: Alto de Bustellán and Alto de la Casa del Puerto Loop, a difficult 33.6 miles (54.0 km) path. This route offers continuous ascents and descents through hilly terrain, providing panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Luarca to Tineo Cycling Route, a 55.5 miles (89.3 km) trail leading through diverse Asturian scenery on well-paved roads, often completed in about 5 hours 50 minutes. Road cycling around Tineo is defined by challenging climbs, lush river valleys, and historic towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on difficult routes. The routes in Tineo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tineo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Tineo?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Tineo, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. The region is known for its challenging and diverse terrain.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Tineo?
A: The road cycling routes in Tineo are predominantly challenging, with 16 routes rated as difficult and 5 as moderate. There are no easy routes listed, indicating the area is best suited for experienced cyclists seeking significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Tineo?
A: You can expect hilly and mountainous terrain with continuous ascents and descents, offering panoramic views. The region is characterized by lush greenery, rolling hills, and river valleys, with many routes utilizing well-paved, low-traffic roads.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Tineo?
A: Yes, Tineo offers several excellent circular road cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078467641/alto-de-la-marta-and-alto-de-porciles-loop-from-navelgas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Marta and Alto de Porciles Loop from Navelgas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 60.3 miles (97.0 km) long and features challenging climbs. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078448058/tineo-alto-de-bustellan-and-alto-de-la-casa-del-puerto-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tineo: Alto de Bustellán and Alto de la Casa del Puerto Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.6 miles (54.0 km) path with continuous ascents and descents.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes offer stunning panoramic views of Asturias' verdant landscapes, lush forests, and river valleys. You'll encounter significant mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2873224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Marta&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6493683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Palo Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Lavadoira&lt;/a&gt;, which provide challenging climbs and rewarding vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the road cycling routes in Tineo?
A: Yes, Tineo is part of the historic Primitive Way of Saint James, so you can explore charming villages and historic towns. Notable landmarks include the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5125287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of St. Mary Major in Salas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4506626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Roque of Tineo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Tineo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, diverse Asturian scenery, and the well-paved, low-traffic roads that make for an engaging experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Tineo?
A: Asturias is known for its verdant landscapes throughout the year. While specific seasonal conditions can vary, the region's lush greenery and diverse scenery make it appealing for cycling during most temperate months. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there options for advanced road cyclists seeking significant challenges?
A: Absolutely. Tineo is ideal for advanced road cyclists. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353804604/luarca-to-tineo-cycling-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca to Tineo Cycling Route&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 55.5 miles (89.3 km) with substantial elevation, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of La Magdalena – Pola de Allande loop from Tinéu&lt;/a&gt;, offer demanding ascents and descents through mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I find cafes, pubs, or accommodation along the road cycling routes in Tineo?
A: Yes, the region features charming villages and historic towns along the routes, providing opportunities to rest and refuel. You'll find local cafes, pubs, and accommodation options, especially in larger towns like Tineo itself and other settlements along the Primitive Way.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists to access routes in Tineo?
A: Tineo is a key point on the Primitive Way of Saint James, which connects several towns. While specific public transport options for cyclists can vary, regional bus services often connect towns in Asturias. It's advisable to check local transport schedules and bicycle carriage policies in advance.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Tineo?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for many routes, including Tineo itself. Look for designated parking areas or inquire locally for the most convenient spots to begin your ride.
tips: Nice picture of the Negro river in the beautiful town of Luarca.
At this point we are 1 km from crowning the Alto de la Casa del Puerto. Precious prints along the road from Pontigón to Tineo. A luxury for road bike lovers.

The name of this town comes from the river that runs through it. From this point we have a magnificent view of the river. In summer if we visit Navelgas we will be able to know some of its traditions. The Gold Panning, which consists of the extraction of the Navelgas River with the use of pans. Gold panning was carried out in the past for artisan mineral exploitation, and currently, due to its low profitability, it is an entertainment tourist activity.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is considered difficult, designed for experienced racebikers. It involves significant elevation changes and challenging climbs, typical of the Asturian landscape. You should have very good fitness to tackle this ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the ride?
faqs.answer: You will primarily ride on paved roads, but be prepared for varied and challenging terrain. The route features numerous mountain passes and winding roads with substantial positive slopes. While mostly paved, some sections might overlap with unpaved or gravel paths if they coincide with Vías Verdes or Camino routes, so be aware of potential surface changes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers diverse scenery and a few notable highlights. You'll pass by the Navelgas River in Navelgas, experience sections of the Pontigón–Tineo Road, and conclude near the Río Negro in Luarca. The journey itself provides stunning views of lush forests, captivating valleys, and picturesque villages.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of other official trails. You'll find it overlaps with parts of the [CIMA AS25] Bustellán, San Antón de Concilleiro, and Los Ḷḷugarinos trails, as well as small portions of the Northern Route (Asturia) and Mtb Northern Route (Asturia).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in Asturias, and therefore this route, is typically during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more stable, avoiding the peak summer heat and potential winter rain or snow in higher elevations. Always check the local forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Navelgas, you can typically find parking within or near the village. Alternatively, if you prefer to start from Luarca, parking options are also available there. We recommend checking local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: No, this racebike route is not suitable for dogs. The long distance, challenging terrain, and high speeds involved in racebiking make it unsafe and impractical to bring a dog along.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and trails in the Asturias region.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should pack essential racebiking gear, including a well-maintained bike, helmet, appropriate cycling apparel, and plenty of water and nutrition. Carry a repair kit, spare tubes, a pump, and a first-aid kit. Due to the varied terrain and potential for changing weather, layers are advisable.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or find accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Both Navelgas and Luarca are villages where you can find amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation. Luarca, being a coastal town, offers more options. While on the route itself, especially in more rural sections, services may be sparse, so plan your stops and carry sufficient supplies.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is explicitly rated as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginner road cyclists. The significant elevation gain (over 2,700 meters) and nearly 90-kilometer distance require a high level of fitness and experience with challenging road cycling terrain.
